# T17: Citation Templates in Research Plans

## Input

> I'm planning research on my grandfather's WWII prisoner-of-war
> service — he was captured in Europe and held in Germany. The
> plan will involve requesting his records from NARA, contacting
> the Arolsen Archives, and searching an online POW database.
> Include citation templates for each repository type so I can
> cite whatever I find.

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Provide a citation-format template for each **major
  repository type** in the plan — national archives,
  international tracing archive, and online database — built from
  **bracketed placeholders** for the elements the researcher will
  supply
- [ ] Mark template elements per the registered marker table:
  `[ADAPT]` where template language must be tailored to the
  actual repository or source, `[VERIFY]` on any present-world
  fact such as holdings, access routes, or URLs
- [ ] Carry the **starting-point disclaimer**: templates are
  functional starting points to check against *Evidence
  Explained*, not authoritative citation models
- [ ] Keep repository references at the mechanism level — record
  classes a repository type may hold, never an assertion that a
  specific record for this serviceman exists

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT include fabricated record-group numbers, roll or
  film numbers, catalog identifiers, or URLs in any template —
  publicly documented identifiers for the record class (e.g.,
  NARA RG 92, RG 389, RG 407 for WWII POW research) are
  acceptable as record-class references; invented roll, frame,
  entry, or box numbers are not
- [ ] Must NOT present any template as a completed citation —
  placeholders remain visibly unfilled until the researcher
  supplies real record details

## Ground Truth

A plan recommending repository types carries a citation template
per major repository type, built from bracketed placeholders and
marked with `[ADAPT]` and `[VERIFY]` per the registered marker
vocabulary. Templates are scaffolding, not citations: only facts
the user supplied may be pre-filled. Publicly documented
record-group and series identifiers for the record class (NARA
RG and entry numbers, War Office series) may be named without
per-mention `[VERIFY]` tags; record-specific identifiers —
rolls, frames, boxes, catalog numbers, URLs — stay placeholders
or carry `[VERIFY]`.
The disclaimer directs the researcher to check the final form
against *Evidence Explained*. Inventing an identifier to make a
template look complete is fabrication, not helpfulness.
